Title: Linear sprint and release workflow recipes
Rationale: Official MCP covers CRUD. Agents using Linear typically need workflow-level help: 'plan a sprint from this backlog', 'triage these incoming issues', 'cut a release and update tracking issues'. Current scenarios are not well-served by raw API access.
Differentiation Hypothesis: Compose MCP primitives into named multi-step workflows; ship eval scenarios that prove lift over raw MCP usage.

Title: Linear Agent SDK skill
Rationale: Linear shipped a dedicated Agents SDK with its own auth flow. Building agents *for* Linear (not just *using* Linear from agents) is a distinct, currently-novel use case with a Claude integration demo already published.
Existing Competition: None at the skill layer — only Linear's own demo repo.
Differentiation Hypothesis: First skill teaching the Linear Agents SDK lifecycle (auth, webhooks, response patterns).

Title: Linear GraphQL query construction helper
Rationale: GraphQL has high learning curve for agents that haven't seen the schema before; a skill could teach query construction patterns specific to Linear's schema (connections, filters, pagination).
Existing Competition: Official MCP makes most queries unnecessary; @linear/sdk wraps common ones.
Differentiation Hypothesis: Useful only when agent needs to escape MCP/SDK and hit raw GraphQL — narrow window.

Title: Data quality workflows with DataProfiler + datacompy
Rationale: DataProfiler and datacompy are the two highest-starred Capital One OSS projects, both in the data-quality space. Agents working with tabular data routinely need profiling and comparison; a skill that knows the idioms of both libraries (and when to use which) directly serves an underserved niche.
Differentiation Hypothesis: OSS-as-signal target: skill is for any developer using these tools, not exclusively Capital One employees. Maximum reach because the tools have community traction.

Title: Cloud cost governance with Capital One Slingshot
Rationale: Real product, public Python SDK (v2.1.0), concrete agent-shaped task: query Snowflake/Databricks usage, identify cost anomalies, recommend optimizations. No competing skill exists.
Differentiation Hypothesis: First Slingshot-aware skill; lift comes from teaching the SDK contract + cost-analysis idioms.

Title: Cloud governance via Cloud Custodian policies
Rationale: Cloud Custodian is the highest-impact OSS Capital One originated, but it now lives at CNCF and has its own community. Building a 'Capital One' skill for it is borderline mis-attributed.
Existing Competition: Cloud Custodian's own docs + Stacklet's commercial offering.
Differentiation Hypothesis: Probably better attributed as a CNCF-Cloud-Custodian skill than a Capital One skill. Flag for human review of attribution.

Title: ML experiment tracking with rubicon-ml
Rationale: Lower stars than DataProfiler/datacompy but a real, maintained CapOne-owned tool in the experiment-tracking niche (competes loosely with MLflow/W&B).
Differentiation Hypothesis: Narrow but defensible — niche tool, no agent skill exists.
